personalInfo.name: Satgun Singh Sodhi
personalInfo.summary: AI/ML Engineer and B.Tech CSE student at VIT Vellore (9.42 CGPA) with hands-on experience in deep learning, LLM systems, computer vision, and production ML infrastructure.

bullets.text: Engineered RMS-normalized flow deltas and cosine similarity features on sliding windows across all pairwise sensor combinations, improving anomaly detection recall by 14% and reducing processing latency by 25%.

Built deep Autoencoder models (SELU, AlphaDropout, MAE loss) for unsupervised anomaly detection across 5 industrial systems, achieving up to 99.47% training accuracy and 93.05% validation accuracy on large multi-sensor time-series data.

Implemented Bayesian hyperparameter optimization from scratch using Gaussian Processes (RBF kernel) and Expected Improvement to auto-tune training parameters, accelerating search efficiency by 3.5x over standard grid search.
Delivered dashboards, technical documentation, and presentations to mentors, leads, and stakeholders, honing cross-functional communication in a production ML environment.

projects.name: AsymPT: Asymmetric Parallel Transformer for Medical Image Segmentation

bullets.text: Designed a hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture with asymmetric placement: CNNs at high resolutions, a Parallel Hybrid Block with Gated Fusion at mid-scale, and Dual-Swin blocks at the bottleneck for global context modelling.
Outperformed TransUNet on the Synapse Multi-Organ benchmark — 84% Dice Score at 28B FLOPs vs 77.48% at 106B FLOPs, achieving a superior efficiency-performance tradeoff.
Deployed a production-ready training pipeline with mixed-precision AMP, deep supervision (3-level weighted loss), cosine annealing LR, and multi-format inference support (NIfTI, PNG, NPY).

Co-designed AsymPT, an asymmetric parallel transformer for Synapse multi-organ CT segmentation (~35-40M parameters, ~28B FLOPs) achieving 84% Dice Score at 28B FLOPs vs TransUNet's 77.48% at 106B FLOPs, delivering a superior efficiency-performance tradeoff.

Incorporated a composite loss function with Focal Loss and label smoothing plus an EMA weight-averaging wrapper to stabilize training, reduce overconfidence, and improve small-organ Dice scores for challenging classes like gallbladder and esophagus.

Deployed a production-ready training pipeline with mixed-precision AMP, deep supervision (3-level weighted loss), cosine annealing LR, and multi-format inference support (NIfTI, PNG, NPY), with an augmentation toolkit including elastic deformations, random scale+crop, and cutout.

projects.name: DasAI: AI-Powered Discord Assistant
projects.techStack: Python, Discord.py, Supabase, PostgreSQL (pgvector), HuggingFace Inference API

bullets.text: Built a self-hostable Discord AI assistant with a RAG pipeline using Supabase pgvector (384-dim embeddings) for semantic knowledge base search with per-server data isolation.

Integrated Sentence Transformers to index messages for similarity-based retrieval, and implemented rolling conversation summaries with Llama-3.2-3B and DuckDuckGo for real-time context-aware responses.
Shipped a Next.js + TypeScript admin dashboard on Vercel with role-based access control and Supabase RLS authentication; bot containerized on Railway via Docker.

Designed and implemented a multi-tenant Discord copilot that stores per-guild configuration, roles, message history, and pgvector-backed RAG documents in Supabase using vector(384) embeddings and IVFFlat cosine indexes for fast similarity search.

Built an HF InferenceClient integration that drives both chat and embedding calls via asyncio executors, runs a classifier sub-prompt to decide when to web-search, and injects DuckDuckGo summaries plus RAG snippets into the final system prompt while enforcing channel allowlists and team-lead/member roles.

Developed an authenticated Next.js admin console using Supabase SSR/CSR clients to manage guild-level prompts, allowed channels, conversation summaries, user roles, and RAG documents, wired into a production Docker + Railway deployment.
Engineered a 5-stage machine vision pipeline converting raw screenshots into structured JSON/Markdown at ~287ms end-to-end latency for downstream LLM consumption.
Fine-tuned RT-DETR on custom UI datasets with a hybrid detector-fallback strategy, extracting bounding boxes, semantic roles, reading order, and confidence scores.
Packaged as a pip-installable Python library with CLI, modular config system, pytest suite, and dedicated fine-tuning scripts for custom dataset adaptation.

Engineered a 5-stage machine vision pipeline converting raw screenshots into structured JSON/Markdown at ~287ms end-to-end latency for downstream LLM consumption, combining RT-DETR object detection with EasyOCR text extraction.
Fine-tuned RT-DETR on custom UI datasets with a hybrid detector-fallback strategy, extracting bounding boxes, semantic roles, reading order, and confidence scores for desktop/mobile UI elements.
Packaged as a pip-installable Python library with CLI, modular config system, pytest suite, and dedicated fine-tuning scripts for custom dataset adaptation.

projects.name: SLM: Financial Sentiment Analysis via Transformer Architecture
projects.techStack: Python, PyTorch, Tiktoken, LoRA, HuggingFace Transformers

bullets.text: Built a transformer-based SLM from scratch in PyTorch using GPT-2 Tiktoken for financial sentiment classification and automated buy/sell/hold trading signal generation.
Applied LoRA for parameter-efficient fine-tuning on FinancialPhraseBank and FiQA, reducing trainable parameters from millions to thousands while preserving domain expressiveness.
Used instruction tuning to align outputs to standardized sentiment categories for robust inference on financial jargon, negations, and implicit market cues.

Built a GPT-style transformer from scratch in PyTorch with tied embeddings, flash-attention-aware causal self-attention, and GELU-based MLP blocks, trained via a two-phase curriculum (general English → financial corpora) with AdamW, warmup+cosine LR, gradient accumulation, and mixed-precision AMP.

Applied LoRA for parameter-efficient fine-tuning on FinancialPhraseBank and FiQA, reducing trainable parameters from millions to thousands while preserving domain expressiveness for sentiment classification and buy/sell/hold signal generation.

Deployed the model as FinPeak.ai, a Streamlit chat app that downloads safetensors weights from HuggingFace, reconstructs GPTConfig, and generates instruction-formatted financial answers with controllable temperature and response length.

projects.name: Smart India Hackathon — Cloud & Network Infrastructure
projects.techStack: AWS EC2, Docker, PostgreSQL, n8n, AWS S3

bullets.text: Deployed Dockerized n8n workflow automation and PostgreSQL services on AWS EC2 with persistent volumes and secure inter-container networking.
Integrated an AWS S3 bucket for scalable cloud storage and delivered end-to-end infrastructure setup under 36-hour hackathon constraints.
Deployed Dockerized n8n workflow automation and PostgreSQL services on AWS EC2 with persistent volumes and secure inter-container networking.
Integrated an AWS S3 bucket for scalable cloud storage and delivered end-to-end infrastructure setup under 36-hour hackathon constraints.

projects.name: Persistent Cloud Tunnel & Automated ML Workflow Engine
projects.techStack: Raspberry Pi, Cloudflare Warp & Tunnel, Docker Compose, n8n

bullets.text: Configured a Raspberry Pi as an always-on edge node with Cloudflare Warp and Tunnel to establish persistent secure connectivity bypassing network outages and captive portals.
Configured Docker Compose with persistent volumes and automated restart policies to keep n8n running continuously with data preserved across reboots.
Configured a Raspberry Pi as an always-on edge node with Cloudflare Warp and Tunnel to establish persistent secure connectivity bypassing network outages and captive portals.
Configured Docker Compose with persistent volumes and automated restart policies to keep n8n running continuously with data preserved across reboots.

projects.name: SHL Conversational Assessment Recommender
projects.techStack: FastAPI, Python, FAISS, Sentence Transformers, OpenRouter LLM

bullets.text: Built a conversational AI recommender using FastAPI and OpenRouter to guide recruiters in choosing assessment tests from a 377-item SHL catalog.
Implemented semantic search using FAISS and Sentence Transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings) to index and retrieve test metadata under 50ms latency.
Designed custom Scope Guards and Response Validators in FastAPI to bound LLM conversational behavior and prevent hallucinations.

Built a conversational AI recommender using FastAPI and OpenRouter that indexes a 377-item SHL catalog via BM25Okapi with enriched search_text strings, injecting detailed and compact catalog contexts into the LLM system prompt.

Implemented semantic search using FAISS and Sentence Transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings) to index and retrieve test metadata under 50ms latency, with a hybrid retrieval strategy prepending curated core assessments to BM25-ranked candidates.

Engineered URL/name validation that auto-corrects hallucinated product references via case-insensitive name lookup and secondary BM25 fallback, with off-topic filtering and Pydantic v2 strict JSON contracts.

projects.name: IQAgent: Local LLM Interview Question Generator
projects.techStack: Python, Ollama, Gemma3:4b, PDF Plumber, CLI

bullets.text: Developed a localized Python CLI tool that parses PDF resumes and generates structured technical interview questions with sample answers using Ollama (Gemma-3-4b).
Integrated target Job Description matching to align generated questions with specific role requirements, enhancing interviewer screening efficacy.
Ensured 100% data privacy of candidate PII by routing all LLM inference through a fully localized, offline Ollama pipeline.

Developed a localized Python CLI tool that parses PDF resumes with pypdf and generates structured technical interview questions with sample answers using Ollama (Gemma-3-4b), ensuring 100% data privacy via fully offline inference.
Integrated target Job Description matching to align generated questions with specific role requirements, with an interactive advanced CLI that captures multi-line JDs from stdin.
Structured output as markdown-friendly text persisted to interview_questions.txt, enabling reuse in interview prep workflows and easy versioning under source control.

projects.name: Fundr: Startup Crowdfunding & Consumer Investment Platform

bullets.text: Developed a functional crowdfunding prototype connecting early-stage startups and consumer investors, supporting dual Rolling and Goal-Based investment models.
Implemented responsive UI dashboards using React 19 and Tailwind CSS to track campaign progress, funding timelines, and equity metrics.
Developed a functional crowdfunding prototype using React 19 + TypeScript with a typed ProjectContext managing project creation and investment flows across three SPA routes.
Implemented responsive UI dashboards using Tailwind CSS to track campaign progress, funding timelines, and equity metrics with dynamic progress bars and localized currency formatting.
Deployed behind a minimal Express server serving the Vite-built frontend, providing clean separation between static SPA hosting and future API endpoints.

projects.name: Outreach-Agent: AI-Driven Resume Customization System
projects.techStack: Java 21, Spring Boot 3.4, LangChain4j, H2, Gmail/Drive API, Thymeleaf

bullets.text: Developed an agentic Spring Boot system using LangChain4j with a 3-tool architecture (knowledge base search, PDF generation, page-length validation) to automatically customize resumes targeting job descriptions.

Implemented a robust batch processing pipeline with an AtomicBoolean concurrency lock to prevent race conditions across parallel LLM invocations, alongside duplicate target prevention and exponential backoff for web scraping.
Engineered a persistent PDF caching system using SHA-256 hashing on job descriptions and scraped company research to bypass redundant LLM generation, saving API costs and reducing latency.
Designed an automated skill sorting algorithm to dynamically align resume keywords with parsed job requirements, eliminating LLM hallucination and enforcing strict ATS compliance.

Developed an agentic Spring Boot system using LangChain4j with a 3-tool architecture (knowledge base search, PDF generation, page-length validation) to automatically customize resumes targeting job descriptions.

Implemented a robust batch processing pipeline with an AtomicBoolean concurrency lock to prevent race conditions across parallel LLM invocations, alongside duplicate target prevention and exponential backoff for web scraping.
Engineered a persistent PDF caching system using SHA-256 hashing on job descriptions and scraped company research to bypass redundant LLM generation, saving API costs and reducing latency.
Designed an automated skill sorting algorithm to dynamically align resume keywords with parsed job requirements, eliminating LLM hallucination and enforcing strict ATS compliance.

projects.name: AI Video Transcription & Summary – Multimodal Meeting Summarizer
projects.techStack: Python, OpenAI Whisper, OpenCV, Ollama (LLaVA + LLaMA 3.2), ffmpeg

bullets.text: Engineered a resumable multimodal summarization CLI that extracts audio via ffmpeg, transcribes with OpenAI Whisper, samples key frames using OpenCV, and calls Ollama vision/text models (LLaVA + LLaMA 3.2) to produce structured markdown summaries.
Implemented per-stage checkpointing (transcript, frames, frame descriptions, summary) with --force overrides, enabling large-file resilience and efficient iterative runs on long meetings.
Packaged with a portable setup.sh that validates Python/ffmpeg/Ollama prerequisites, creates a venv, and pulls required Ollama models for one-command bootstrap.

Engineered a resumable multimodal summarization CLI that extracts audio via ffmpeg, transcribes with OpenAI Whisper, samples key frames using OpenCV, and calls Ollama vision/text models (LLaVA + LLaMA 3.2) to produce structured markdown summaries.
Implemented per-stage checkpointing (transcript, frames, frame descriptions, summary) with --force overrides, enabling large-file resilience and efficient iterative runs on long meetings.
Packaged with a portable setup.sh that validates Python/ffmpeg/Ollama prerequisites, creates a venv, and pulls required Ollama models for one-command bootstrap.
Engineered a command-line interface in Python for execution of GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE requests with parameterized header and payload options.
Implemented response parsing mechanisms to automatically format and output clean headers and JSON bodies directly to log files.

projects.name: django-News-API: API-Key Protected News Aggregation Service
projects.techStack: Django 5, Django REST Framework, SQLite, Python

bullets.text: Implemented a Django 5 + DRF API with path-parameterized endpoints and manual API-key validation, supporting list, search, random, and mode-based news retrieval with a custom scoring function.
Designed a relational schema with News, Comment, apiKey, and Log models, using ModelSerializers for JSON responses while serving traditional HTML templates for authentication.
Encoded 'latest', 'popular', and 'best' selection logic as dedicated aggregation functions, with per-article view counters and access logs for observability.

Implemented a Django 5 + DRF API with path-parameterized endpoints and manual API-key validation, supporting list, search, random, and mode-based news retrieval with a custom 'best' scorer combining recency (0.3 weight) and popularity (0.7 weight).
Designed a relational schema with News, Comment, apiKey, and Log models, using ModelSerializers for JSON responses while serving traditional HTML templates for authentication and browsing.
Encoded 'latest', 'popular', and 'best' selection logic as dedicated aggregation functions over SQLite, with per-article view counters and human-readable access logs in a Log table for observability.

projects.name: Iqilo: Gamified Tech Roadmap Learning OS
projects.techStack: Next.js, TypeScript, TailwindCSS v4, useSyncExternalStore, localStorage

bullets.text: Architected a Next.js/TypeScript learning OS where immutable course roadmaps and day-level units are wired into an external ProgressStore via useSyncExternalStore, supporting instant 'continue where you left off' redirects based on lastVisitedDay per course.

Built a gamification subsystem that derives XP levels, streaks, and badges from localStorage-backed stores, synchronizing changes across tabs via storage and custom events while keeping UI reactive through external-store subscriptions.

Implemented a fully client-side progress API exposing day/resource toggles, per-week/per-course completion stats, JSON export, and auto-redirect logic alongside a Tailwind-driven dashboard with recommended quests, certificate cards, and learning velocity graphs.

projects.name: nvidia-claude-code-bridge: Rate-Limited LLM Proxy for NVIDIA NIM + Claude Code
projects.techStack: Python, litellm, asyncio, NVIDIA NIM, Claude Code, Rate Limiting

bullets.text: Implemented a per-model async rate limiter (ModelRateLimiter + AsyncRateLimiter) around litellm that enforces a 40 RPM cap using a lock-protected timestamp deque, preventing downstream NVIDIA NIM 429 responses.

Built a multimodal safety layer in async_pre_call_hook that clamps max_tokens and strips image payloads from messages for text-only models, injecting a dummy text block and logging explicit warnings when Claude Code sends images to non-vision backends.

Engineered a failure introspection system that detects HTTP 429 rate-limit conditions, dumps exception attributes, NIM response headers (retry-after/limit fields), and raw/parsed JSON bodies to enable rapid debugging of bridge failures.
Served as Point of Contact for CodeHub hackathon and anchored 5+ technical events including a keynote with Love Babbar (500+ attendees).
Achieved 1st Place at Binary Battles at Gravitas, VIT's annual technical fest.

extracurriculars.organization: Lllama3 Hackathon: Local Medical LLM Prototype

bullets.text: Prototyped an end-to-end local medical assistant by downloading a quantized 8B-parameter med-llama model from HuggingFace, registering it in Ollama, and wiring it into LlamaIndex for offline inference.
Built a Gradio + Plotly HealthPulse UI that logs health events into a pandas DataFrame and renders interactive time-series plots for the local med-LLM.
Delivered the prototype under hackathon time constraints using colab-xterm for Ollama process management directly from Jupyter notebooks.

Prototyped an end-to-end local medical assistant by downloading a quantized 8B-parameter med-llama .gguf model from HuggingFace, registering it in Ollama, and wiring it into LlamaIndex for offline inference inside Colab.
Built a Gradio + Plotly HealthPulse UI that logs health events into a pandas DataFrame and renders interactive time-series plots, providing a front-end for the local med-LLM.
Delivered the prototype under hackathon time constraints using colab-xterm for Ollama process management directly from Jupyter notebooks.
